messages.content: You are a Lean 4 autoformalization agent. Generate a small ordered sequence of Lean declarations for the provided LaTeX theorem-like unit. Use only the source text, selector plan, previous-project context if shown, and Lean-checked Mathlib signatures in the prompt. The original aligned Lean declarations, names, statements, and proofs are withheld. Return exactly one JSON object.

{
  "benchmark_policy": {
    "gold_comparison_is_posthoc_only": true,
    "posthoc_alignment_hidden": true,
    "target_lean_available_to_generator": false
  },
  "instructions": [
    "Do not use sorry, admit, placeholders, or comments standing in for proof.",
    "Do not include import statements or markdown fences.",
    "Do not ask for or infer the hidden aligned Lean declaration names/statements/proofs.",
    "Treat selector_unchecked_statement_sketch as non-authoritative mathematical intent only; do not copy its Lean syntax verbatim.",
    "Follow the Lean-checked signatures exactly when they differ from the selector's expected shape.",
    "Use the actual Lean-checked argument order from hydrated_mathlib_context. If the selector expected shape conflicts with the checked signature, the checked signature wins.",
    "If a selected Mathlib API is field-specific, unit-specific, or otherwise not strong enough for the source statement, say so in notes and generate only declarations justified by the visible context.",
    "Do not invent project helper names that are not shown in available_prior_project_context, needed_project_context, or source context.",
    "When available_prior_project_context contains Lean snippets for project definitions or predicates, use those exact names and statement shapes instead of rephrasing the source with raw hypotheses.",
    "Prefer a narrow declaration sequence over a broad bundled conjunction when the source unit decomposes into multiple Lean declarations.",
    "Keep theorem-local assumptions explicit rather than relying on unavailable global variables.",
    "Every identifier used in a theorem statement must be introduced by an explicit binder, local declaration, or imported/opened declaration. In particular, source parameters such as coefficient types, matrix sizes, rings, variables, and typeclass assumptions must be bound in the generated theorem, e.g. `{K : Type*} [CommRing K] {n : ℕ}` when the source quantifies over a commutative ring and a natural number.",
    "If you cannot produce a complete proof from visible context, set status to cannot_prove_from_visible_context and leave lean_file_body empty. Never output Lean containing sorry/admit/placeholders."
  ],
  "lean_environment": {
    "default_opens_used_for_context_hydration": [
      "open scoped Polynomial BigOperators",
      "open PowerSeries Finset"
    ],
    "imports_available_in_verification_file": [
      "Mathlib"
    ],
    "toolchain": "leanprover/lean4:v4.28.0"
  },
  "required_json_schema": {
    "units": [
      {
        "declaration_names": [
          "names introduced in lean_file_body"
        ],
        "lean_file_body": "complete Lean declarations only; no imports or markdown",
        "notes": [
          "brief caveats or remaining uncertainty"
        ],
        "status": "generated|cannot_prove_from_visible_context",
        "unit_key": "unit-001",
        "used_context": [
          "source/project/Mathlib facts actually used"
        ]
      }
    ]
  },
  "task": "Produce Lean declarations for each planned declaration task. The output should be a small Lean file body, without imports, containing the ordered declarations needed for this one source unit.",
  "units": [
    {
      "context_pack_size_risk": "low",
      "formalization_risks": [
        "source ambiguity: none",
        "missing notation: none",
        "broad multi-part theorem: single statement"
      ],
      "planned_declarations": [
        {
          "available_prior_project_context": [
            {
              "project_declarations": [
                {
                  "context_source": "referencing_prior_declaration",
                  "declared_source_labels": [],
                  "imports": [
                    "Mathlib"
                  ],
                  "kind": "theorem",
                  "lean_snippet": "/-- The determinant formula from Definition def.det.det.\n    Note: Mathlib uses A(σ(i), i) rather than A(i, σ(i)). -/\ntheorem det_eq_sum_sign_prod (A : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) K) :\n    A.det = ∑ σ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n), Equiv.Perm.sign σ • ∏ i, A (σ i) i := by\n  rw [Matrix.det_apply]",
                  "line_range": [
                    104,
                    108
                  ],
                  "module": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.DeterminantsBasic",
                  "name": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.Det.det_eq_sum_sign_prod",
                  "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/DeterminantsBasic.lean",
                  "referenced_source_labels": [
                    "def.det.det"
                  ],
                  "snippet_truncated": false
                },
                {
                  "context_source": "referencing_prior_declaration",
                  "declared_source_labels": [],
                  "imports": [
                    "Mathlib"
                  ],
                  "kind": "theorem",
                  "lean_snippet": "/-- Alternative form with A(i, σ(i)) matching the textbook.\n    This is obtained by substituting σ ↦ σ⁻¹ in the Mathlib definition.\n\n    This is the formalization of Definition def.det.det from the source:\n    det A = ∑_{σ ∈ Sₙ} (-1)^σ · ∏_{i=1}^n A_{i,σ(i)}\n\n    The Mathlib definition uses A(σ(i), i) rather than A(i, σ(i)), but these\n    are equivalent by substituting σ ↦ σ⁻¹. -/\ntheorem det_eq_sum_sign_prod_textbook (A : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) K) :\n    A.det = ∑ σ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n), Equiv.Perm.sign σ • ∏ i, A i (σ i) := by\n  rw [Matrix.det_apply]\n  -- Substitute σ ↦ σ⁻¹: since σ ↦ σ⁻¹ is a bijection on Sₙ, the sum is unchanged\n  rw [← Equiv.sum_comp (Equiv.inv (Equiv.Perm (Fin n)))]\n  congr 1\n  ext σ\n  congr 1\n  · -- sign(σ⁻¹) = sign(σ)\n    simp only [Equiv.inv_apply, Equiv.Perm.sign_inv]\n  · -- ∏ i, A (σ⁻¹ i) i = ∏ i, A i (σ i) by reindexing via σ\n    rw [← Equiv.prod_comp σ]\n    simp\n\n/-!\n## Examples of small determinants\n\nFor n = 0: det() = 1 (empty product)\nFor n = 1: det(a) = a\nFor n = 2: det [[a, b], [a', b']] = a·b' - b·a'\nFor n = 3: The 6-term expansion over S₃\n-/",
                  "line_range": [
                    110,
                    139
                  ],
                  "module": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.DeterminantsBasic",
                  "name": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.Det.det_eq_sum_sign_prod_textbook",
                  "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/DeterminantsBasic.lean",
                  "referenced_source_labels": [
                    "def.det.det"
                  ],
                  "snippet_truncated": false
                }
              ],
              "reason": "Earlier or explicitly referenced source unit with project declarations. These declarations are prior context, not aligned target declarations for the selected unit.",
              "source_labels": [
                "def.det.det"
              ],
              "source_unit_id": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/Determinants/BasicProperties.tex:def.det.det"
            },
            {
              "project_declarations": [
                {
                  "context_source": "aligned_prior_declaration",
                  "declared_source_labels": [
                    "exa.det.hollow5x5"
                  ],
                  "imports": [
                    "Mathlib"
                  ],
                  "kind": "theorem",
                  "lean_snippet": "/-- A matrix with a \"hollow core\" of zeros has determinant zero.\n    Label: exa.det.hollow5x5\n\n    More precisely: if A_{i,j} = 0 whenever i, j ∈ {1, 2, 3} (0-indexed),\n    then det A = 0. This is because any permutation σ must have some\n    i ∈ {1,2,3} with σ(i) ∈ {1,2,3} by pigeonhole. -/\ntheorem det_hollow_core_eq_zero (A : Matrix (Fin 5) (Fin 5) K)\n    (hA : ∀ i j : Fin 5, i.val ∈ ({1, 2, 3} : Set ℕ) → j.val ∈ ({1, 2, 3} : Set ℕ) →\n      A i j = 0) : A.det = 0 := by\n  rw [det_apply]\n  apply Finset.sum_eq_zero\n  intro σ _\n  -- It suffices to show the product is zero\n  suffices h : ∏ i, A (σ i) i = 0 by simp [h]\n  -- By pigeonhole, there exists i with both i and σ(i) in the middle\n  obtain ⟨i, hi_mid, hσi_mid⟩ := exists_middle_to_middle σ\n  -- So A (σ i) i = 0\n  apply Finset.prod_eq_zero (mem_univ i)\n  exact hA (σ i) i hσi_mid hi_mid\n\n/-!\n## Proposition: det(xᵢyⱼ) = 0 (Proposition prop.det.xiyj)\n\nFor n ≥ 2, if we form the n×n matrix with (i,j)-entry xᵢ·yⱼ,\nthen its determinant is 0.\n\nThe proof uses ∑_{σ ∈ Sₙ} (-1)^σ = 0 for n ≥ 2.\n-/",
                  "line_range": [
                    213,
                    240
                  ],
                  "module": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.DeterminantsBasic",
                  "name": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.Det.det_hollow_core_eq_zero",
                  "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/DeterminantsBasic.lean",
                  "referenced_source_labels": [],
                  "snippet_truncated": false
                }
              ],
              "reason": "Earlier or explicitly referenced source unit with project declarations. These declarations are prior context, not aligned target declarations for the selected unit.",
              "source_labels": [
                "exa.det.hollow5x5"
              ],
              "source_unit_id": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/Determinants/BasicProperties.tex:exa.det.hollow5x5"
            },
            {
              "project_declarations": [
                {
                  "context_source": "aligned_prior_declaration",
                  "declared_source_labels": [
                    "prop.det.xiyj"
                  ],
                  "imports": [
                    "Mathlib"
                  ],
                  "kind": "def",
                  "lean_snippet": "/-- The matrix with entries x_i * y_j.\n    Label: prop.det.xiyj -/\ndef outerProductMatrix (x y : Fin n → K) : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) K :=\n  Matrix.of fun i j => x i * y j",
                  "line_range": [
                    242,
                    245
                  ],
                  "module": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.DeterminantsBasic",
                  "name": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.Det.outerProductMatrix",
                  "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/DeterminantsBasic.lean",
                  "referenced_source_labels": [],
                  "snippet_truncated": false
                },
                {
                  "context_source": "aligned_prior_declaration",
                  "declared_source_labels": [
                    "prop.det.xiyj"
                  ],
                  "imports": [
                    "Mathlib"
                  ],
                  "kind": "theorem",
                  "lean_snippet": "/-- Determinant of outer product matrix is 0 for n ≥ 2.\n    Label: prop.det.xiyj -/\ntheorem det_outerProduct_eq_zero (x y : Fin n → K) (hn : 2 ≤ n) :\n    (outerProductMatrix x y).det = 0 := by\n  -- outerProductMatrix is the same as vecMulVec\n  have h : outerProductMatrix x y = vecMulVec x y := rfl\n  rw [h]\n  -- Use Mathlib's det_vecMulVec which requires Nontrivial (Fin n)\n  haveI : Nontrivial (Fin n) := Fin.nontrivial_iff_two_le.mpr hn\n  exact det_vecMulVec x y",
                  "line_range": [
                    292,
                    301
                  ],
                  "module": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.DeterminantsBasic",
                  "name": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.Det.det_outerProduct_eq_zero",
                  "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/DeterminantsBasic.lean",
                  "referenced_source_labels": [],
                  "snippet_truncated": false
                },
                {
                  "context_source": "aligned_prior_declaration",
                  "declared_source_labels": [
                    "prop.det.xiyj"
                  ],
                  "imports": [
                    "Mathlib"
                  ],
                  "kind": "theorem",
                  "lean_snippet": "/-- Second proof of det_outerProduct_eq_zero using matrix factorization.\n    The matrix (xᵢyⱼ) factors as A·B where A has only first column nonzero\n    and B has only first row nonzero. Since n ≥ 2, A has a zero column,\n    so det(A) = 0.\n    Label: prop.det.xiyj (second proof) -/\ntheorem det_outerProduct_eq_zero' (x y : Fin n → K) (hn : 2 ≤ n) :\n    (outerProductMatrix x y).det = 0 := by\n  -- outerProductMatrix is definitionally equal to vecMulVec\n  -- For n ≥ 2, Fin n is nontrivial, so Mathlib's det_vecMulVec applies\n  have : Nontrivial (Fin n) := Fin.nontrivial_iff_two_le.mpr hn\n  exact det_vecMulVec x y",
                  "line_range": [
                    679,
                    689
                  ],
                  "module": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.DeterminantsBasic",
                  "name": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.Det.det_outerProduct_eq_zero'",
                  "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/DeterminantsBasic.lean",
                  "referenced_source_labels": [],
                  "snippet_truncated": false
                }
              ],
              "reason": "Earlier or explicitly referenced source unit with project declarations. These declarations are prior context, not aligned target declarations for the selected unit.",
              "source_labels": [
                "prop.det.xiyj"
              ],
              "source_unit_id": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/Determinants/BasicProperties.tex:prop.det.xiyj"
            },
            {
              "project_declarations": [
                {
                  "context_source": "aligned_prior_declaration",
                  "declared_source_labels": [
                    "prop.det.xi+yj"
                  ],
                  "imports": [
                    "Mathlib"
                  ],
                  "kind": "def",
                  "lean_snippet": "/-- The matrix with entries x_i + y_j.\n    Label: prop.det.xi+yj -/\ndef sumMatrix (x y : Fin n → K) : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) K :=\n  Matrix.of fun i j => x i + y j",
                  "line_range": [
                    321,
                    324
                  ],
                  "module": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.DeterminantsBasic",
                  "name": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.Det.sumMatrix",
                  "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/DeterminantsBasic.lean",
                  "referenced_source_labels": [],
                  "snippet_truncated": false
                },
                {
                  "context_source": "aligned_prior_declaration",
                  "declared_source_labels": [
                    "prop.det.xi+yj"
                  ],
                  "imports": [
                    "Mathlib"
                  ],
                  "kind": "theorem",
                  "lean_snippet": "/-- Determinant of sum matrix is 0 for n ≥ 3.\n    Label: prop.det.xi+yj\n\n    The proof uses matrix factorization: (x_i + y_j) = A * B where A has a zero column. -/\ntheorem det_sumMatrix_eq_zero (x y : Fin n → K) (hn : 3 ≤ n) :\n    (sumMatrix x y).det = 0 := by\n  rw [sumMatrix_eq_factorA_mul_factorB x y (by omega : 2 ≤ n)]\n  rw [det_mul]\n  rw [det_sumMatrix_factorA_eq_zero x hn]\n  ring\n\n\n/-!\n## Theorem: Transposes preserve determinants (Theorem thm.det.transp)\n\ndet(A^T) = det(A)\n-/",
                  "line_range": [
                    405,
                    421
                  ],
                  "module": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.DeterminantsBasic",
                  "name": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.Det.det_sumMatrix_eq_zero",
                  "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/DeterminantsBasic.lean",
                  "referenced_source_labels": [],
                  "snippet_truncated": false
                },
                {
                  "context_source": "aligned_prior_declaration",
                  "declared_source_labels": [
                    "prop.det.xi+yj"
                  ],
                  "imports": [
                    "Mathlib"
                  ],
                  "kind": "theorem",
                  "lean_snippet": "/-- Second proof of det_sumMatrix_eq_zero using matrix factorization.\n    The matrix (xᵢ + yⱼ) factors as A·B where A has only first two columns\n    nonzero and B has only first two rows nonzero. Since n ≥ 3, A has a\n    zero column, so det(A) = 0.\n    Label: prop.det.xi+yj (second proof) -/\ntheorem det_sumMatrix_eq_zero' (x y : Fin n → K) (hn : 3 ≤ n) :\n    (sumMatrix x y).det = 0 :=\n  det_sumMatrix_eq_zero x y hn",
                  "line_range": [
                    691,
                    698
                  ],
                  "module": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.DeterminantsBasic",
                  "name": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.Det.det_sumMatrix_eq_zero'",
                  "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/DeterminantsBasic.lean",
                  "referenced_source_labels": [],
                  "snippet_truncated": false
                },
                {
                  "context_source": "referencing_prior_declaration",
                  "declared_source_labels": [],
                  "imports": [
                    "Mathlib"
                  ],
                  "kind": "def",
                  "lean_snippet": "/-- The matrix A in the factorization of sumMatrix: first column is x_i, second column is 1, rest are 0.\n    Used in the second proof of prop.det.xi+yj. -/\ndef sumMatrix_factorA (x : Fin n → K) : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) K :=\n  Matrix.of fun i j =>\n    if j.val = 0 then x i\n    else if j.val = 1 then 1\n    else 0",
                  "line_range": [
                    330,
                    336
                  ],
                  "module": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.DeterminantsBasic",
                  "name": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.Det.sumMatrix_factorA",
                  "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/DeterminantsBasic.lean",
                  "referenced_source_labels": [
                    "prop.det.xi+yj"
                  ],
                  "snippet_truncated": false
                },
                {
                  "context_source": "referencing_prior_declaration",
                  "declared_source_labels": [],
                  "imports": [
                    "Mathlib"
                  ],
                  "kind": "def",
                  "lean_snippet": "/-- The matrix B in the factorization of sumMatrix: first row is 1, second row is y_j, rest are 0.\n    Used in the second proof of prop.det.xi+yj. -/\ndef sumMatrix_factorB (y : Fin n → K) : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) K :=\n  Matrix.of fun i j =>\n    if i.val = 0 then 1\n    else if i.val = 1 then y j\n    else 0",
                  "line_range": [
                    338,
                    344
                  ],
                  "module": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.DeterminantsBasic",
                  "name": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.Det.sumMatrix_factorB",
                  "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/DeterminantsBasic.lean",
                  "referenced_source_labels": [
                    "prop.det.xi+yj"
                  ],
                  "snippet_truncated": false
                }
              ],
              "reason": "Earlier or explicitly referenced source unit with project declarations. These declarations are prior context, not aligned target declarations for the selected unit.",
              "source_labels": [
                "prop.det.xi+yj"
              ],
              "source_unit_id": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/Determinants/BasicProperties.tex:prop.det.xi+yj"
            }
          ],
          "hydrated_mathlib_context": [
            {
              "exact_identifier": "Matrix.det_transpose",
              "expected_signature_or_shape": "det_transpose : det (Aᵀ) = det A",
              "lean_check": {
                "messages": [
                  {
                    "data": "Matrix.det_transpose.{v, u_2} {n : Type u_2} [DecidableEq n] [Fintype n] {R : Type v} [CommRing R] (M : Matrix n n R) :\n  M.transpose.det = M.det",
                    "line": 4,
                    "severity": "information"
                  }
                ],
                "signature": "Matrix.det_transpose.{v, u_2} {n : Type u_2} [DecidableEq n] [Fintype n] {R : Type v} [CommRing R] (M : Matrix n n R) :\n  M.transpose.det = M.det",
                "status": "checked"
              },
              "query": "Matrix.det_transpose",
              "request_index": 1,
              "source_part": "whole unit",
              "task_id": "unit-001-task-1",
              "unit_key": "unit-001",
              "why_needed": "core theorem for determinant of transpose"
            }
          ],
          "kind": "theorem",
          "missing_or_uncertain_context": [],
          "needed_project_context": [
            {
              "name": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.Det.det_eq_sum_sign_prod_textbook",
              "why_needed": "provides the textbook determinant formula used in proof"
            }
          ],
          "needed_source_context": [
            "thm.det.transp: Let n ∈ ℕ. If A ∈ K^{n×n} is any n×n-matrix, then det(A^T) = det A."
          ],
          "selector_sketch_warning": "This selector sketch is mathematical intent only. Do not copy its Lean syntax or argument order verbatim; Lean-checked hydrated signatures are authoritative.",
          "selector_unchecked_statement_sketch": "For any square matrix A over a commutative ring K, the determinant of its transpose equals its determinant.",
          "source_part": "whole unit",
          "task_id": "unit-001-task-1"
        }
      ],
      "previous_source_context": [
        {
          "reason": "Earlier or explicitly referenced source theorem-like unit.",
          "source_unit": {
            "environment": "definition",
            "id": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/Determinants/BasicProperties.tex:def.det.det",
            "labels": [
              "def.det.det"
            ],
            "line_range": [
              85,
              103
            ],
            "parse_warnings": [],
            "part_markers": [],
            "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/Determinants/BasicProperties.tex",
            "referenced_labels": [
              "def.perm.sign"
            ],
            "source_text": "\\begin{definition}\n\\label{def.det.det}Let $n\\in\\mathbb{N}$. Let $A\\in K^{n\\times n}$ be an\n$n\\times n$-matrix. The \\emph{determinant} $\\det A$ of $A$ is defined to be\nthe element%\n\\[\n\\sum_{\\sigma\\in S_{n}}\\left(  -1\\right)  ^{\\sigma}\\underbrace{A_{1,\\sigma\n\\left(  1\\right)  }A_{2,\\sigma\\left(  2\\right)  }\\cdots A_{n,\\sigma\\left(\nn\\right)  }}_{=\\prod\\limits_{i=1}^{n}A_{i,\\sigma\\left(  i\\right)  }}%\n\\]\nof $K$. Here, as before:\n\n\\begin{itemize}\n\\item we let $S_{n}$ denote the $n$-th symmetric group (i.e., the group of\npermutations of $\\left[  n\\right]  =\\left\\{  1,2,\\ldots,n\\right\\}  $);\n\n\\item we let $\\left(  -1\\right)  ^{\\sigma}$ denote the sign of the permutation\n$\\sigma$ (as defined in Definition \\ref{def.perm.sign}).\n\\end{itemize}\n\\end{definition}"
          }
        },
        {
          "reason": "Earlier or explicitly referenced source theorem-like unit.",
          "source_unit": {
            "environment": "example",
            "id": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/Determinants/BasicProperties.tex:exa.det.hollow5x5",
            "labels": [
              "exa.det.hollow5x5"
            ],
            "line_range": [
              195,
              212
            ],
            "parse_warnings": [],
            "part_markers": [],
            "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/Determinants/BasicProperties.tex",
            "referenced_labels": [],
            "source_text": "\\begin{example}\n\\label{exa.det.hollow5x5}Let $a,b,c,d,e,\\ldots,p$ be $16$ elements of $K$.\nProve that%\n\\[\n\\det\\left(\n\\begin{array}\n[c]{ccccc}%\na & b & c & d & e\\\\\np & 0 & 0 & 0 & f\\\\\no & 0 & 0 & 0 & g\\\\\nn & 0 & 0 & 0 & h\\\\\nm & l & k & j & i\n\\end{array}\n\\right)  =0.\n\\]\n(The \\textquotedblleft$o$\\textquotedblright\\ is a letter \\textquotedblleft\noh\\textquotedblright, not a zero. Not that it matters much...)\n\\end{example}"
          }
        },
        {
          "reason": "Earlier or explicitly referenced source theorem-like unit.",
          "source_unit": {
            "environment": "proposition",
            "id": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/Determinants/BasicProperties.tex:prop.det.xiyj",
            "labels": [
              "prop.det.xiyj"
            ],
            "line_range": [
              312,
              320
            ],
            "parse_warnings": [],
            "part_markers": [],
            "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/Determinants/BasicProperties.tex",
            "referenced_labels": [],
            "source_text": "\\begin{proposition}\n\\label{prop.det.xiyj}Let $n\\in\\mathbb{N}$ be such that $n\\geq2$. Let\n$x_{1},x_{2},\\ldots,x_{n}\\in K$ and $y_{1},y_{2},\\ldots,y_{n}\\in K$. Then,%\n\\[\n\\det\\left(  \\left(  x_{i}y_{j}\\right)  _{1\\leq i\\leq n,\\ 1\\leq j\\leq\nn}\\right)  =0.\n\\]\n\n\\end{proposition}"
          }
        },
        {
          "reason": "Earlier or explicitly referenced source theorem-like unit.",
          "source_unit": {
            "environment": "proposition",
            "id": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/Determinants/BasicProperties.tex:prop.det.xi+yj",
            "labels": [
              "prop.det.xi+yj"
            ],
            "line_range": [
              409,
              417
            ],
            "parse_warnings": [],
            "part_markers": [],
            "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/Determinants/BasicProperties.tex",
            "referenced_labels": [],
            "source_text": "\\begin{proposition}\n\\label{prop.det.xi+yj}Let $n\\in\\mathbb{N}$ be such that $n\\geq3$. Let\n$x_{1},x_{2},\\ldots,x_{n}\\in K$ and $y_{1},y_{2},\\ldots,y_{n}\\in K$. Then,%\n\\[\n\\det\\left(  \\left(  x_{i}+y_{j}\\right)  _{1\\leq i\\leq n,\\ 1\\leq j\\leq\nn}\\right)  =0.\n\\]\n\n\\end{proposition}"
          }
        }

        "source_text": "\\begin{theorem}\n[Transposes preserve determinants]\\label{thm.det.transp}Let $n\\in\\mathbb{N}$.\nIf $A\\in K^{n\\times n}$ is any $n\\times n$-matrix, then $\\det\\left(\nA^{T}\\right)  =\\det A$.\n\\end{theorem}"
